transcript.clean_text: sunday saw the last of the three stages in hungary at this 105th judo italia coming a day after the time trial in the capital budapest that had been won by simon yates and seeing matteo von de paul take pink for a second consecutive day fodder pool would be in pink for that third stage yates leader in the clubhouse as far as the gc is concerned albert in phoenix this time though looking to win with jakub marichko their italian sprinter fernando gabiria was back in form heading into this race mark cavadish returning to the jittered italia for the first time in nine whole years and 201 kilometers separated the peloton from start to finish in caposvir and balaton food two intermediate sprints along the way and one very late climb not testing the legs too much though t hoin a fourth category effort just 12 kilometers from the finish line one of the flatter stages in this jira italia should have been one nailed on for the sprinters the breakaway was nailed on with the very first pedal stroked it took no longer than 150 meters of the race for three riders to go off the front and two of them were the same riders we'd tin in the break on day one rivie was up there from aolo cometa with two riders from drawn hopper taliani and ice behind everybody could shoot the breeze and calm down it'll be a relatively slow day first bit of action came at the intermediate sprint taliani winning ahead of rivie third place to bailey's before the five points for the maya chiclamino available behind were taken by fernando gabidia arnold de mar was looking good he finished in second place in that mini sprint nizolo and germai were up there as well with 75 kilometers to go the gap was two minutes and as it came down to 25 seconds samuel river attacked taliani would be dropped by east though would continue to work the two men left in the breakaway would be caught with just under 28 kilometers to go and then the nurse would build as the gc riders tried to stay at the front and those after sprint wins would also try to occupy that same real estate looking for the climb there were three points available for the fourth category pascal encore who was in second position in the king of the mouth in standings tried to get away rick zarbel supposedly ready for a lead-out role for giacomo nizolo would go after him to kind keep the jersey but it will be a cornered take the three points both will be level at the top of the standings inghorn would stay out the front for a few kilometers getting as many as 10 seconds of a gap but he'd be brought back in with six k's to go and it was time for the first bunch sprint of this gilo d'italia three kilometers to go and alpacine fenix appeared mature from the pool in lead out mode this time with a kilometer to go quick step out for final were on the front it had been four long years since they won a stage at the jittered italia having other ambitions the pink jersey and gc prospects since then but mark cavendish was back and he was looking to take a first stage win at the georgia d'italia on his first appearance in nine years 15 down the years until this point he hit the front with 300 meters to go perhaps it was early it wasn't kavadish was on fine form continuing his sprint nobody able to get around him and mark cavadish with win number 160 in his career becoming the third oldest stage winner in the history of the tour of italy the greatest sprinter ever winning a game quick step after vinyl on fire cavendish at the front then led out by marco amal dimar on the right hand side of this shot would finish second fernando gabiria would be up there on the podium with them as well binyam girmai and jakob marico making up the top five arms in the air again for mark cavendish at almost 37 years of age once more a stage winner at the judo only two riders have won more grand tour stages than him now down the years eddie merks and mario chipolini matcha von der paul would keep the pink jersey as we head to italy his lead of 11 seconds over simon yates 16 in front of tom dumoulin will be tested with the next stage to mount etna so a trip to sicily on the rest day on monday the race resumes on tuesday stage four a four-star stage on our first serious mountaintop finish an opening opportunity for bigger gaps in the gc it hasn't always exploded and erupted on etna this time there's a chance for things to be different sprints in paterno and bianca villa the second one bringing bonus seconds and an over 20 kilometer climb to the summit at nicolosi refugio sapienza the race will be live on gcm plus eurosport and discovery plus we hope you'll join us for coverage from start to finish every single day at this year's judo italian
